output: T4 completed. The `control_loop.py` async boundary is now mapped: the file currently has 21 `self.runpod` references total, of which 19 are method calls and all 19 are inside `async def` functions. The 18 line numbers called out in the batch prompt were cross-checked individually and every enclosing function is async. I also found drift from the plan metadata: there is one additional async method call at line 1380 (`_terminate_worker`), plus a sync constructor assignment at line 44 and an async attribute read at line 1303. Practical consequence for T5–T7: every adapter method replacing current `self.runpod` calls should be `async def`, and every current call site will need `await` except `generate_worker_id()` and the plain `gpu_type` attribute read. Script audit is also complete: `spawn_gpu.py`, `shutdown_all_workers.py`, `terminate_single_worker.py`, and `test_runpod.py` already use `asyncio.run(...)` at the CLI boundary; `ssh_to_worker.py`, `spot_instance_lifetime_test.py`, `uv_e2e_test.py`, and `uv_lock_benchmark.py` have main guards but no `asyncio.run(...)` yet and will need wrappers once migrated. The sync helper modules `scripts/debug/commands/storage.py`, `scripts/debug/services/workers.py`, and likely `scripts/debug/commands/runpod.py` will need internal sync wrappers around new async calls.
commands_run: python - <<'PY' ... AST audit for plan line numbers in gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py ... PY

for f in scripts/spawn_gpu.py scripts/ssh_to_worker.py scripts/shutdown_all_workers.py scripts/terminate_single_worker.py scripts/test_runpod.py scripts/spot_instance_lifetime_test.py scripts/uv_e2e_test.py scripts/uv_lock_benchmark.py scripts/debug/commands/storage.py scripts/debug/services/workers.py scripts/debug/commands/runpod.py; do rg -n 'if __name__ ==|asyncio\.run\(|create_runpod_client|RunpodClient|self\.runpod\.|runpod_lifecycle|gpu_orchestrator\.runpod' "$f"; done

task_updates.executor_notes: Async/sync audit completed against `gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py` and the target scripts. Current `self.runpod` usage in `control_loop.py` has drifted from the plan metadata: there are 21 total `self.runpod` references in the file today, comprising 19 method calls plus 1 constructor assignment (`__init__` line 44) and 1 attribute read (`self.runpod.gpu_type` at line 1303). All 19 method-call sites are inside `async def` functions. The 18 approximate lines called out in the task (386, 448, 465, 1126, 1205, 1301, 1306, 1336, 1342, 1480, 1502, 1517, 1541, 1544, 1557, 1598, 1600, 1651) were cross-checked and every enclosing function is async: `_handle_early_termination`, `_handle_spawning_workers`, `_mark_worker_error`, `_collect_worker_diagnostics`, `_spawn_worker`, `_check_storage_health`, `_check_error_worker_cleanup`, `_failsafe_stale_worker_check`, `_efficient_zombie_check`. Additional drift found: line 1380 in `_terminate_worker` is another async `self.runpod.terminate_worker(...)` call not listed in the task prompt; line 44 (`self.runpod = create_runpod_client()`) is sync `__init__`; line 1303 is an async attribute read used by `create_worker_record`. Practical contract for T5-T7: all adapter methods that replace current method calls must be `async def`, and every current call site will need `await` except `generate_worker_id()` and the plain `gpu_type` attribute read. Script audit: the target script entrypoints are sync-shaped overall. Files already using `asyncio.run(...)` at the CLI boundary: `scripts/spawn_gpu.py`, `scripts/shutdown_all_workers.py`, `scripts/terminate_single_worker.py`, `scripts/test_runpod.py`. Files with `if __name__ == '__main__'` but no current `asyncio.run(...)`, so they will need wrappers once migrated to async package/adapter calls: `scripts/ssh_to_worker.py`, `scripts/spot_instance_lifetime_test.py`, `scripts/uv_e2e_test.py`, `scripts/uv_lock_benchmark.py`. Non-CLI sync helper modules that will need internal `asyncio.run(...)` (or equivalent sync wrapper) around new async calls when edited in T6: `scripts/debug/commands/storage.py`, `scripts/debug/services/workers.py`, and likely `scripts/debug/commands/runpod.py` if switched to `runpod_lifecycle.list_pods/find_orphans`.
